cards.title: Sports Medicine — Exercise Physiology Foundations

alerts.label: Sports Medicine in Naturopathic Practice
alerts.text: Sports and exercise medicine optimises athletic performance, prevents and manages injuries, and supports recovery. Naturopathic sports medicine integrates evidence-based nutritional strategies, botanical ergogenic aids, hydrotherapy, manual therapy, and mental performance coaching within the framework of holistic athlete assessment. Key principle: the athlete is a whole person — physical, psychological, nutritional, and recovery dimensions must all be addressed.

rows: Creatine monohydrate 3–5g/day ★★★ (increases PCr stores by 20%). Loading: 20g/day × 5 days optional
Beta-alanine 3.2–6.4g/day ★★★ (buffers lactate via carnosine ↑). Sodium bicarbonate 0.3g/kg pre-event (acute lactate buffer)
Carbohydrate periodisation, iron adequacy (aerobic capacity), Vit D, CoQ10, beet juice (nitrate → NO → O₂ efficiency)
Longest lifespan category — 5× lower all-cause mortality vs sedentary
Sedentary = 5× higher mortality than elite — greater risk factor than smoking

cards._legacyHtml:
Sports Medicine in Naturopathic Practice
Sports and exercise medicine optimises athletic performance, prevents and manages injuries, and supports recovery. Naturopathic sports medicine integrates evidence-based nutritional strategies, botanical ergogenic aids, hydrotherapy, manual therapy, and mental performance coaching within the framework of holistic athlete assessment. Key principle: the athlete is a whole person — physical, psychological, nutritional, and recovery dimensions must all be addressed.
Energy SystemDurationPrimary FuelSportsNaturopathic Optimisation
ATP-PCr (Phosphagen)0–10 secondsPhosphocreatineSprinting, Olympic lifting, jumpingCreatine monohydrate 3–5g/day ★★★ (increases PCr stores by 20%). Loading: 20g/day × 5 days optional
Glycolytic (Anaerobic)10 sec – 2 minMuscle glycogen → lactate400m, 800m, rowing, team sports intervalsBeta-alanine 3.2–6.4g/day ★★★ (buffers lactate via carnosine ↑). Sodium bicarbonate 0.3g/kg pre-event (acute lactate buffer)
Oxidative (Aerobic)>2 minutesGlycogen + fats + protein (minor)Marathon, cycling, triathlon, swimmingCarbohydrate periodisation, iron adequacy (aerobic capacity), Vit D, CoQ10, beet juice (nitrate → NO → O₂ efficiency)
VO₂max Categoryml/kg/min (Male)ml/kg/min (Female)Longevity Implication
Elite athlete>60>55Longest lifespan category — 5× lower all-cause mortality vs sedentary
High fitness50–6045–55Excellent longevity profile
Average35–5030–45Moderate longevity benefit
Below average / sedentary<35<30Sedentary = 5× higher mortality than elite — greater risk factor than smoking

cards.title: Sports Nutrition — Naturopathic Performance Protocol

rows: ↑ PCr stores → ↑ ATP resynthesis → ↑ high-intensity performance. Also: anti-inflammatory, neuroprotective
Safe long-term. Minor weight gain (water). Kidney disease: use with caution
300–500ml beet juice or 6–8 mmol nitrate, 2–3hrs pre-event
Dietary nitrate → NO → ↓ O₂ cost of exercise → improved endurance economy by 3–5%
Pink/red urine (nitrate effect — benign). Less effective in elite athletes (already efficient)
↑ Muscle carnosine → ↑ intracellular pH buffer → delays fatigue in anaerobic efforts
Paraesthesia (tingling) — harmless, reduced by sustained-release form
Adenosine receptor antagonist → ↓ perceived exertion, ↑ endurance, ↑ strength 1–3%
Sleep disruption if taken <6hrs before sleep. Dependence/tolerance. Avoid >9mg/kg (adverse effects)
Adaptogen — ↓ cortisol, ↑ testosterone, ↑ VO₂max, ↑ recovery between sessions
Well tolerated. Avoid in pregnancy. Thyroid conditions: monitor TSH

steps.title: Macronutrient Timing
steps.text: Pre-exercise (2–3hrs before): mixed meal — carbohydrate (1–3g/kg), moderate protein (0.3g/kg), low fat, low fibre. 30–60 min before: easily digestible carbs (banana, white rice, dates). During exercise (>60 min): 30–60g carbohydrate/hour (gels, dates, raisins, sports drink). Post-exercise (within 30 min — anabolic window): 20–40g protein + carbohydrate 1–1.5g/kg (insulin spike drives amino acid uptake). Protein synthesis window stays elevated for 48hrs post-resistance training.

steps.title: Protein Requirements for Athletes
steps.text: Endurance athletes: 1.2–1.4g/kg/day. Strength/power athletes: 1.6–2.2g/kg/day. Masters athletes (>50): 1.8–2.4g/kg/day (anabolic resistance — higher protein needed). Leucine threshold: 3–4g leucine per meal to maximally stimulate MPS (muscle protein synthesis). Leucine-rich sources: whey protein (★ fastest — post-workout), casein (slow-release — pre-sleep), eggs, chicken, fish, lentils + complemented grains. Plant athletes: higher total protein needed (lower leucine density) — combine legumes + grains + seeds per meal.

steps.title: Hydration & Electrolytes
steps.text: Sweat loss: 0.5–2.5L/hr depending on intensity and heat. 2% bodyweight loss in sweat = 20% performance reduction. Urine colour chart: pale yellow = adequate, dark = dehydrated. Electrolyte replacement for >60 min exercise: sodium 500–700mg/hr (prevents hyponatraemia — dangerous in marathon runners who over-drink water without sodium). Coconut water (natural electrolyte — potassium rich, low sodium — better for lower-intensity activities). Oral rehydration salts for severe dehydration/heat illness.

Ergogenic AidEvidenceDoseMechanismCautions
Creatine Monohydrate★★★ ISSN Grade A3–5g/day maintenance (loading 20g/day × 5d optional)↑ PCr stores → ↑ ATP resynthesis → ↑ high-intensity performance. Also: anti-inflammatory, neuroprotectiveSafe long-term. Minor weight gain (water). Kidney disease: use with caution
Beetroot/Nitrate★★★ Grade A for endurance300–500ml beet juice or 6–8 mmol nitrate, 2–3hrs pre-eventDietary nitrate → NO → ↓ O₂ cost of exercise → improved endurance economy by 3–5%Pink/red urine (nitrate effect — benign). Less effective in elite athletes (already efficient)

Ashwagandha★★★ Grade A for VO₂max300–600mg BD (KSM-66)Adaptogen — ↓ cortisol, ↑ testosterone, ↑ VO₂max, ↑ recovery between sessionsWell tolerated. Avoid in pregnancy. Thyroid conditions: monitor TSH

cards.title: Sports Injury — Naturopathic Recovery Protocol

rows: Inflammation — necessary for healing. Do NOT over-suppress.
Pain control, limit excessive swelling (not all inflammation)

POLICE protocol (Protection, Optimal Load, Ice 10-15min/hr, Compression, Elevation). Bromelain 500mg TID (anti-oedema). Arnica topical ★★. Avoid NSAIDs if possible — they delay tendon and bone healing by inhibiting Cox-2-mediated prostaglandins needed for repair

Collagen peptides 15g/day + Vit C 500mg (1hr before exercise — load in the repair window). Vit C 1g/day (collagen hydroxylation). Zinc 30mg/day (metalloproteinase activity). Turmeric 500mg TID (anti-inflammatory without suppressing healing). Progressive loading begins.
Ensure full tissue competency before return to sport

Eccentric loading protocols (tendon remodelling — Alfredson protocol for Achilles). Progressive sport-specific loading. Proprioception training. Omega-3 3g/day (anti-inflammatory, supports satellite cell activation). Collagen maintenance 10g/day.

pearls.label: 🟡 RED-S — Relative Energy Deficiency in Sport
pearls.text: RED-S (formerly Female Athlete Triad) affects both male and female athletes. Triad in females: Low Energy Availability (LEA) → Menstrual Dysfunction → Low Bone Density. In males: LEA → ↓ testosterone → low bone density, performance decline. LEA occurs when caloric intake is insufficient for exercise demands — often unintentional in endurance athletes. LEAF-Q questionnaire for screening. Management: caloric intake ↑ 250–500 kcal/day, dietitian input, reduce training load temporarily, bone protection (Vit D + calcium), address psychological factors (often perfectionism, body dysmorphia). Serious condition — bone stress fractures, cardiac arrhythmia, infertility, suppressed immunity.

cards._legacyHtml:
Injury PhaseTimelineNaturopathic PriorityInterventions
Acute (0–72hrs)Inflammation — necessary for healing. Do NOT over-suppress.Pain control, limit excessive swelling (not all inflammation)POLICE protocol (Protection, Optimal Load, Ice 10-15min/hr, Compression, Elevation). Bromelain 500mg TID (anti-oedema). Arnica topical ★★. Avoid NSAIDs if possible — they delay tendon and bone healing by inhibiting Cox-2-mediated prostaglandins needed for repair
Subacute (3 days–3 weeks)Proliferative — fibroblast activity, collagen depositionOptimise tissue repair qualityCollagen peptides 15g/day + Vit C 500mg (1hr before exercise — load in the repair window). Vit C 1g/day (collagen hydroxylation). Zinc 30mg/day (metalloproteinase activity). Turmeric 500mg TID (anti-inflammatory without suppressing healing). Progressive loading begins.
Remodelling (3 weeks–12 months)Collagen remodelling — strength restorationEnsure full tissue competency before return to sportEccentric loading protocols (tendon remodelling — Alfredson protocol for Achilles). Progressive sport-specific loading. Proprioception training. Omega-3 3g/day (anti-inflammatory, supports satellite cell activation). Collagen maintenance 10g/day.
🟡 RED-S — Relative Energy Deficiency in Sport
RED-S (formerly Female Athlete Triad) affects both male and female athletes. Triad in females: Low Energy Availability (LEA) → Menstrual Dysfunction → Low Bone Density. In males: LEA → ↓ testosterone → low bone density, performance decline. LEA occurs when caloric intake is insufficient for exercise demands — often unintentional in endurance athletes. LEAF-Q questionnaire for screening. Management: caloric intake ↑ 250–500 kcal/day, dietitian input, reduce training load temporarily, bone protection (Vit D + calcium), address psychological factors (often perfectionism, body dysmorphia). Serious condition — bone stress fractures, cardiac arrhythmia, infertility, suppressed immunity.

cards.title: Mental Performance & Recovery Science

rows: Caffeine 3–6mg/kg, L-theanine 200mg (caffeine synergy — reduces jitter), lion's mane mushroom 500–1000mg/day (NGF stimulation), adequate sleep (most powerful focus optimiser)
★★★ Caffeine + L-theanine combination: superior to caffeine alone for sustained attention
Ashwagandha KSM-66 300mg BD (chronic), L-theanine 200mg (acute), mindfulness-based performance enhancement (MBPE), breathing (4-7-8 technique), visualisation
★★★ Mindfulness: well-established for performance anxiety in elite athletes

7–9 hrs is minimum. Sleep extension to 10hrs: +9% sprint speed, ↑ reaction time, ↑ free throw accuracy (Stanford basketball study). Tart cherry juice 250ml BD ★★★ (melatonin + anti-inflammatory — reduces muscle soreness + improves sleep). Magnesium glycinate 400mg. No screens 90 min before bed.
★★★ Tart cherry: reduced muscle soreness, improved recovery in endurance athletes

Cold water immersion 10–15°C × 10 min (reduces DOMS ★★★). Contrast hydrotherapy (1 min cold : 2 min hot × 5 cycles). Tart cherry. Omega-3. Turmeric. Sleep ★★★. Active recovery (low-intensity movement 20 min).
★★★ Cold water immersion: most evidence-based recovery modality for team sport athletes
